findings.control_name: Public Access Block Must Be Enabled
findings.control_description: S3 buckets must have the public access block fully enabled. When disabled, the bucket has no safety net against accidental public exposure from policy or ACL changes. This detects the enabling condition for public access, not the exposure itself.

evidence.why_now: Resource has been unsafe for 240 hours (threshold: 0 hours). Unsafe since 2026-01-01T00:00:00Z.

remediation.description: Public Access Block is not fully enabled. The bucket has no safety net against accidental public exposure from policy or ACL changes.
remediation.action: Enable all four Public Access Block settings on the bucket: BlockPublicAcls, IgnorePublicAcls, BlockPublicPolicy, RestrictPublicBuckets.

findings.control_name: PHI Buckets Must Use SSE-KMS with Customer-Managed Key
findings.control_description: S3 buckets tagged with data-classification=phi must use SSE-KMS encryption with a customer-managed key (CMK), not the default AWS-managed key or SSE-S3. This ensures the organization controls key rotation, access policies, and audit logging for PHI data at rest.

evidence.why_now: Resource has been unsafe for 240 hours (threshold: 0 hours). Unsafe since 2026-01-01T00:00:00Z.

remediation.description: PHI bucket is not using SSE-KMS with a customer-managed key. The organization lacks control over key rotation, access policies, and audit logging for PHI data at rest.
remediation.action: Change the bucket default encryption to SSE-KMS and specify a customer-managed KMS key ARN. Ensure the KMS key policy grants access only to authorized principals. Enable KMS key rotation.

findings.control_description: S3 buckets with any non-public data classification must use SSE-KMS encryption with a customer-managed key, not SSE-S3 (AES256). AES256 uses AWS-managed keys with no customer control over key rotation, access policies, or audit trails. This fires on all classified data except explicitly public or non-sensitive buckets.

evidence.why_now: Resource has been unsafe for 240 hours (threshold: 0 hours). Unsafe since 2026-01-01T00:00:00Z.

remediation.description: Bucket with classified data is using SSE-S3 (AES256) instead of SSE-KMS. AWS-managed keys provide no customer control over key rotation, access policies, or audit trails.
remediation.action: Change the bucket default encryption to SSE-KMS with a customer-managed key. Re-encrypt existing objects by copying them in place with the new encryption settings.

findings.control_description: S3 buckets must have a data-classification tag. Without this tag, tag-conditional invariants for PHI, PII, confidential data, backup integrity, and compliance retention cannot evaluate — the bucket silently passes all sensitivity-gated checks regardless of actual content.

evidence.why_now: Resource has been unsafe for 240 hours (threshold: 0 hours). Unsafe since 2026-01-01T00:00:00Z.

remediation.description: Bucket is missing the data-classification tag. Without this tag, sensitivity-gated invariants cannot evaluate and the bucket silently passes all data-protection checks.
remediation.action: Add a data-classification tag to the bucket with an appropriate value (e.g., phi, pii, confidential, internal, public, non-sensitive). Update your tagging policy to require this tag on all S3 buckets.

findings.control_description: S3 buckets must not allow public read access. Detects buckets with anonymous read exposure via policy or ACL.

evidence.why_now: Resource has been unsafe for 240 hours (threshold: 0 hours). Unsafe since 2026-01-01T00:00:00Z.

remediation.description: Bucket has public read access enabled via policy or ACL. Anyone on the internet can access objects in this bucket.
remediation.action: Enable S3 Public Access Block (all four settings). Remove any bucket policy statements granting access to Principal "*". Remove any ACL grants to AllUsers or AuthenticatedUsers.

findings.control_name: No Public S3 Buckets With Sensitive Data
findings.control_description: S3 buckets tagged with sensitive data classifications (PHI, PII, confidential) must not allow any public access.

evidence.why_now: Resource has been unsafe for 240 hours (threshold: 0 hours). Unsafe since 2026-01-01T00:00:00Z.

remediation.description: A bucket containing sensitive data (PHI, PII, or confidential) has public read or list access. This data is accessible to anyone on the internet.
remediation.action: Immediately enable S3 Public Access Block (all four settings). Remove bucket policy statements granting access to Principal "*". Remove ACL grants to AllUsers or AuthenticatedUsers. Audit CloudTrail logs for unauthorized access during the exposure window.

findings.control_description: S3 bucket policies must not grant public read access.

evidence.why_now: Resource has been unsafe for 240 hours (threshold: 0 hours). Unsafe since 2026-01-01T00:00:00Z.

remediation.description: Bucket policy grants public read (Principal \"*\" with read action).
remediation.action: Remove or constrain the public policy statement. Use restrictive principals or conditions and keep Public Access Block enabled.

findings.control_description: S3 bucket policies must not grant anonymous object listing.

evidence.why_now: Resource has been unsafe for 240 hours (threshold: 0 hours). Unsafe since 2026-01-01T00:00:00Z.

remediation.description: Bucket policy grants public listing (Principal \"*\" with list action).
remediation.action: Remove or constrain policy statements allowing s3:ListBucket to anonymous principals.

findings.control_description: S3 buckets must not allow anonymous listing of objects. Public listing exposes object keys, enabling targeted data exfiltration.

evidence.why_now: Resource has been unsafe for 240 hours (threshold: 0 hours). Unsafe since 2026-01-01T00:00:00Z.

remediation.description: Bucket allows anonymous listing of objects. Public listing exposes all object keys, enabling targeted data exfiltration.
remediation.action: Remove bucket policy statements that grant s3:ListBucket to Principal "*". Remove ACL grants that allow READ to AllUsers. Enable S3 Public Access Block.

findings.control_name: Anonymous S3 Listing Must Be Explicitly Intended
findings.control_description: Anonymous bucket listing increases exposure surface even when objects are public by design. Listing must be explicitly intended via tag.

evidence.why_now: Resource has been unsafe for 240 hours (threshold: 0 hours). Unsafe since 2026-01-01T00:00:00Z.

remediation.description: Bucket has public listing enabled without an explicit intent tag. Anonymous listing increases exposure surface even for public content.
remediation.action: If listing is intentional, add the tag public_list_intended=true to the bucket. Otherwise, remove the policy or ACL granting s3:ListBucket to Principal "*" or AllUsers.

findings.control_name: Protected Prefixes Must Not Be Publicly Readable
findings.control_description: S3 bucket prefixes marked as protected must not be publicly readable. Evaluates bucket policies, ACL grants, and public access block settings to determine effective public read access for each protected prefix. Customize the prefix lists below to match your bucket layout.

evidence.why_now: Protected prefix "backups/" is publicly readable via missing_evidence.

remediation.action: Review the unsafe configuration and remediate.
